steps.title: Factor out the common expression
steps.content:
Both terms on the left side contain $(x+2)$. Factor it out:
$$\frac{1}{6}(x+2)-\frac{1}{5}(x+2)=-5$$
$$(x+2)\left(\frac{1}{6}-\frac{1}{5}\right)=-5$$

steps.title: Simplify the fraction coefficient
steps.content:
Calculate $\frac{1}{6}-\frac{1}{5}$ using a common denominator of 30:
$$\frac{1}{6}-\frac{1}{5} = \frac{5}{30}-\frac{6}{30} = -\frac{1}{30}$$

The equation becomes:
$$(x+2)\left(-\frac{1}{30}\right)=-5$$

steps.title: Solve for x
steps.content: Divide both sides by $-\frac{1}{30}$ (equivalent to multiplying by $-30$): $$x+2 = \frac{-5}{-\frac{1}{30}} = -5 \cdot (-30) = 150$$ Subtract 2 from both sides: $$x = 150 - 2 = 148$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
